detaileddescription.para: Definition of the configuration version 0 in the last number indicates default version

briefdescription.para: Definition of the device name.
Definition of the device type.
Definition of the acceleration sensor name.
Definition of the acceleration sensor type.
Definition of the configuration file name and location.
Definition of the maximum configuration size.

detaileddescription.para: Definition of FRAM raw data storage time when diplacement calculation is enabled
Definition of FRAM raw data storage time when diplacement calculation is disabled

briefdescription.para: Definition of the miniumum activation threshold string.
Definition of the maxiumum activation threshold string.
Definition of the maxiumum deactivation threshold string.
Definition of the miniumum activation threshold.
Definition of the maxiumum activation threshold.
Definition of the maxiumum deactivation threshold.
Definition of the miniumum activation/deactivation difference.
Definition of float value for uninitialized variables.
Acceleration sensor ADXL372.
Number of sensors mentioned in dynamic configuration.
Enumeration mention the sensors to be configured dynamically.
Enumeration mention the sensors to be configured dynamically.
Structure for general related dynamic configuration.
Structure for sensor related dynamic configuration.
Structure for metrics related dynamic configuration.
Structure for displacement related dynamic configuration.
Structure for datalog related dynamic configuration.
Header file of Config class.

detaileddescription.para:
Project
AXO Track Firmware 2023
(c) 2023 AXO Track GmbH, Berlin. All rights reserved.
AXO Track GmbH, Berlin (Germany)
Detailed Description
JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data- interchange format. It is easy for humans to read and write. It is easy for machines to parse and generate. It is based on a subset of the JavaScript Programming Language Standard ECMA-262 3rd Edition - December 1999. JSON is a text format that is completely language independent but uses conventions that are familiar to programmers of the C-family of languages, including C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, Perl, Python, and many others. These properties make JSON an ideal data-interchange language. JSON is built on two structures:
A collection of name/value pairs. In various languages, this is realized as an object, record, struct, dictionary, hash table, keyed list, or associative array.
An ordered list of values. In most languages, this is realized as an array, vector, list, or sequence. These are universal data structures. Virtually all modern programming languages support them in one form or another. It makes sense that a data format that is interchangeable with programming languages also be based on these structures. In JSON, they take on these forms: An object is an unordered set of name/value pairs. An object begins with '{' (left brace) and ends with '}' (right brace). Each name is followed by ':' (colon) and the name/value pairs are separated by ',' (comma).
Source: https://www.json.org/json-en.html A simple example based on the used configuration structure: example config structure /group/entry index/list of key:value pairs
/general/0/name /general/0/type /general/0/version
/sensors/0/name /sensors/0/type /sensors/0/actthres /sensors/0/deactthres
/metrics/0/interval
/displacement/0/b1
/datalog/0/dispmodeoff /datalog/0/rawtransferon /datalog/0/disptransferon /datalog/0/rms_thres
json representation { "general": [ { "name": "axo2", "type": "9160", "version": "0.7.1" } ], "sensors": [ { "name": "adxl372", "type": "accel", "actthres": "2000" // assume x,y,and z activation thresholds are allways equal "deactthres": "2500" // assume x,y,and z deactivation thresholds are allways equal } ], "metrics": [ { "interval" : "120" // in minutes = every 2h } ], "displacement": [ { "b1" : "1.0" // beautification factor } ], "datalog": [ { "dispmodeoff" : "false", "rawtransferon" : "true", "disptransferon" : "true", "rms_thres" : "65", "max_raw_per_day" : "5" } ] }

simplesect.para: This source code is confidential and proprietary information of AXO Track GmbH, Berlin. You may not disclose, copy, or distribute this source code or any part thereof without the explicit permission of AXO Track GmbH, Berlin.
